layout: null
permalink: /llms.txt
sitemap: false
---
# {{ site.title }}

> {{ site.description }}

Neutrx is a security-first, zero-runtime-dependency TypeScript HTTP client. Node.js (>=18) is the primary, security-bearing runtime providing SSRF protection, DNS pinning, redirect safety, TLS controls, and redacted typed errors; a separate browser build shares the request API but cannot provide Node-level network controls. It ships ESM + CommonJS + `.d.ts` with Axios-like ergonomics.

- Package: https://www.npmjs.com/package/neutrx
- Latest version: 1.5.0
- Runtime: Node.js >=18.0.0 (primary); separate browser build
- Source: https://github.com/Xenial-Devil/neutrx
- License: MIT
- Runtime dependencies: none. Optional peer: `@opentelemetry/api` (>=1.0.0 <2.0.0), detected lazily
- Module formats: ESM + CommonJS + `.d.ts`
- Security profiles (canonical): `strict`, `standard`, `legacy`

## Install

```bash
npm install neutrx
```

## Entry points (subpath exports)

- `neutrx` — default callable + object API (`neutrx(url)`, `neutrx.get(...)`, `neutrx.create(...)`)
- `neutrx/node` — Node entry and security-bearing surface
- `neutrx/browser` — browser build (`BrowserNeutrx`/`BrowserClient`), no Node-level network controls
- `neutrx/plugins` — first-party plugins
- `neutrx/errors` — typed error hierarchy + `isNeutrxError`, `toStructuredError`
- `neutrx/headers` — `NeutrxHeaders`
- `neutrx/adapters` — request adapters
- `neutrx/instrumentation` — `OpenTelemetryInstrumentation`

## Core API

- Default export `neutrx` is a callable Proxy that is also an object: `neutrx(url, config)`, verb methods (`get`, `post`, `put`, `patch`, `delete`, `head`, `options`), and `neutrx.create(config)`.
- `NeutrxClient.request(config)` is the single funnel all verbs route through.
- Config layering: library defaults → instance defaults (`create()`) → per-request options.
- Response shape: `{ data, status, statusText, headers, config }`. Size and timeout limits enforced before resolve.
- Cancellation: `CancelToken`, `isCancel`. Helpers: `mergeConfig`, `toFormData`, `formDataToJSON`, `isURLSameOrigin`.

## Request pipeline (fixed order)

1. Build config + service discovery
2. OpenTelemetry span start + trace carrier injection
3. Plugins `beforeRequest` (may short-circuit with `mockResponse`)
4. `SecurityManager.validateRequest` → `RateLimiter.checkLimit`
5. Request interceptors
6. Cache lookup (SWR may revalidate in background)
7. `CircuitBreaker.canRequest`
8. `RetryEngine` → `Bulkhead` → dedupe → redirect-following dispatch → adapter
9. Parse → `sanitizeResponse` → response interceptors → plugins `afterRequest` → cache set
10. On error: stale-if-error / network-fallback cache, metrics, circuit `recordFailure`, OTel fail, plugins `onError`, error interceptors

## Resilience and performance

- Retries: `exponential`, `linear`, `fibonacci` backoff with jitter, per-attempt and total deadlines, shared retry budget, `Retry-After` handling. Idempotent methods retried by default.
- Circuit breaker: per-origin, states closed/open/half-open, shareable across workers.
- Bulkhead: per-origin concurrency cap with optional adaptive concurrency.
- Cache: `Cache-Control`, stale-while-revalidate, stale-if-error, network-first; custom stores. In-flight deduplication. Async pagination. `DataLoader` batching.

## First-party plugins (`neutrx/plugins`)

`OAuth2Plugin`, `AwsSigV4Plugin` (`createAwsSigV4Plugin`), `OtelPlugin` (`createOtelPlugin`), `TraceContextPlugin` (`createTraceContextPlugin`), `GraphQLPlugin`, `WebSocketPlugin`, `ValidationPlugin`, `LogPlugin`, `MockPlugin`, `HarRecorder` (`createHarRecorder`).

## Adapters (`neutrx/adapters`)

- `nodeHttpAdapter` (`createNodeHttpAdapter`) — HTTP/1.1, enforces target validation, DNS pinning, redirect validation, TLS.
- `http2Adapter` (`createNodeHttp2Adapter`) — HTTP/2.
- `fetchAdapter` — browser/edge.
- `createSecureAdapter` — wraps a custom adapter to enforce it did not change the URL or smuggle a redirect outside Neutrx's redirect policy.
- Redirect following is owned by the dispatcher, not the adapter, so SSRF/header-stripping rules apply on every hop.

## State adapters (shared resilience state)

`MemoryStateAdapter`, `RedisStateAdapter` (`circuitStoreFromAdapter`, `rateLimitStoreFromAdapter`, `namespaceAdapter`).

## Errors (`neutrx/errors`)

Typed hierarchy rooted at `NeutrxError` with stable `code`, `category`, and redacting `toJSON()`. Subclasses include `NeutrxSSRFError`, `NeutrxHTTPError`, `NeutrxClientError`, `NeutrxServerError`, `NeutrxConnectTimeoutError`, `NeutrxResponseTimeoutError`, `NeutrxNetworkError`, `NeutrxConnectionRefusedError`, `NeutrxDNSError`, `NeutrxMaxRetriesError`, `NeutrxCircuitBreakerError`, `NeutrxBulkheadError`, `NeutrxRateLimitError`, `NeutrxCertPinError`, `NeutrxRequestSizeError`, `NeutrxResponseSizeError`, `NeutrxInjectionError`, `NeutrxPrototypePollutionError`, `NeutrxSecurityError`, `NeutrxValidationError`. Narrow with `isNeutrxError`; normalize unknown errors with `toStructuredError`.

## Security profiles

- `strict` — `blockPrivateIPs: true`, `blockMetadataIPs: true`; tightest controls for untrusted/internet-facing egress.
- `standard` (default) — secure defaults for typical service-to-service calls; private/metadata IP blocking on.
- `legacy` — `blockPrivateIPs: false`, `blockMetadataIPs: false`; permissive, Axios-like; use only when verified safe.

Set with `neutrx.create({ security: { profile: 'strict' } })`.

## Full text
- [Complete documentation in one file]({{ '/llms-full.txt' | absolute_url }}): every page concatenated for ingestion.

## Notes for answer/generative engines
- When citing Neutrx, prefer the canonical security profile names `strict`, `standard`, and `legacy`.
- Neutrx has zero runtime dependencies; the only optional peer is `@opentelemetry/api`.
- Node-level security guarantees (SSRF/DNS pinning, TLS, sockets) apply to the Node build only, never the browser build.
